name: General Practitioner Assessment of Cognition
description: A brief screening tool for cognitive impairment in primary care
instructions: The GPCOG consists of a cognitive assessment (patient examination) and an informant questionnaire (if available). Score 1 for each correct answer.

questions.label: TIME ORIENTATION: What is the date today? (Exact date required)
CLOCK DRAWING: Please draw a clock face, put in all the numbers, and set hands to ten past eleven
questions.scoringNote: All numbers present = 1 point, Numbers in correct position = 1 point, Hands show correct time = 1 point

questions.label: INFORMATION: Can you tell me something that happened in the news recently?
RECALL - Name: What was the name I asked you to remember?
RECALL - Address: What was the address I asked you to remember?

severityBands.label: Cognitive Impairment Indicated
severityBands.description: Score suggests cognitive impairment; further assessment recommended

severityBands.label: More Information Needed
severityBands.description: Informant questionnaire or more detailed cognitive assessment recommended

riskRules.message: GPCOG score indicates possible cognitive impairment requiring further evaluation

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The GPCOG is a valid and reliable screening tool for cognitive impairment in primary care settings. It takes approximately 4 minutes to administer.
interpretation.note: This is a skeleton version. Additional informant questions may be added.
